base_case: Posecai v. Wal-Mart Stores, Inc., 752 So. 2d 762 (La. 1999)
constructor: Venkateshwar Reddy Jambula (drafted with Claude Opus as programming and writing assistant)

perturbations.original_text: The foreseeability of premises-safety incidents at this specific store location, based on prior incident history at this store and in the immediate neighborhood.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether this consideration (probability of harm) supports or opposes the outcome is treated by the court as neither clearly established nor clearly refuted on this record; the factor is present but is not developed as a load-bearing element of the analysis.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Auto-generated template rewrite for factor f1 (probability_of_harm). Human review recommended before final dataset admission.

perturbations.original_text: The economic impact on the defendant and similarly situated businesses of imposing a duty to provide enhanced premises security — across the many Wal-Mart stores nationally, the cost of such measures is substantial.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether this consideration (burden of precaution) supports or opposes the outcome is treated by the court as neither clearly established nor clearly refuted on this record; the factor is present but is not developed as a load-bearing element of the analysis.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Auto-generated template rewrite for factor f2 (burden_of_precaution). Human review recommended before final dataset admission.

perturbations.original_text: The incentive effect of imposing duty — whether holding the store liable would meaningfully deter future premises-safety incidents or would simply shift liability without preventing harm.
